nodes.title: TextEncodeQwenImageEditPlus (Positive)
widgets_values: Without changing the pose, remove the person's hair, clothing and accessories, and make them completely naked. Make the skin white and the background white.

nodes.title: Cache Notes
widgets_values:
These are the filepaths and filenames for the cache files. You NEED to update the "Cache Full File Path".

Don't need to change the other ones.

nodes.title: Rotation Quadrants
widgets_values:
This controls rotation / unrotation of the image. It is restricted to whole 90 degree rotations. 0 means no rotation. Numbers should be between 0 and 3. Negative numbers don't work.

Rotating the image may improve the RePose output.
The "Edit Model Reference Method" nodes above are not needed if you use Comfy files, but may be needed if you use repackaged ones from other people.

nodes.title: KSampler settings
widgets_values:
You can test and find the best setting by yourself. The following table is for reference.
|   | Qwen | Comfy | lightning LoRA |
|--------|---------|------------|---------------------------|
| Steps  | 40      | 20         | 4                         |
| CFG    | 4.0     | 4.0       | 1.0                       |

By default, we use 20 steps as we just don't want it to take too long. Try 40 if you want a better result, but it will take longer.

nodes.title: SDXL Refine Denoise Control
widgets_values:
The point of the workflow is to get back some style after the Qwen Repose.

Denoising helps you fine-tune till you get a desired result. You should be happy with all previous steps and have tried randomizing seeds already before you try to fine-tune denoise.

Values should be between 0 and 1.0.  The values change depending on the style you're using and how much you want to restore.

widgets_values: 1girl, solo, realistic (eyes, nose), short white hair, bobcut, fluffy hair, white mouse ears, red eyes, collarbone, cleavage, medium breasts, toned body, athletic, perfect hands, skin indentation,

nodes.title: SDXL Pose to Control Nets
widgets_values:
This right here is the raw SDXL Pose image, you can feed it into the the SDPose Estimation.

If your character is nude or wearing skin-tight clothing, you can also pass the SDXL Pose image into the AIO Aux Depth Preprocessor.

The default for both is the Qwen Repose image, since that is consistent. The SDXL will have stronger style and pose, but no clothing.

It is recommended to feed both Depth and Open pose the same image, either SDXL Pose or Qwen RePose, if they are significantly different.

<== This reroute node

nodes.title: Cam, Light, Mood, Scene Tags (Rear)
widgets_values: fashion photoshoot, serious mood, simple white background, depth of field, volumetric lighting,

nodes.title: Quality Tags (Front)
widgets_values: masterpiece, best quality, amazing quality, very aesthetic, high resolution, ultra-detailed, absurdres, newest,

nodes.title: Edge Buffer Notes
widgets_values:
You may need to adjust this.

Torso needs to take up a significant portion of the image, centered as well.
Should range between 5% and 15%.

If arms and legs outstretched, lower the value.

If posture is compact, like a fetal position or squatting, raise the value.

If the head and torso are taking up too little or too much space, it will cause more characters to appear in the background.

nodes.title: Stance Tags
widgets_values: looking at viewer, standing, relaxed pose, hands to side, front,  full body shot,

nodes.title: Detail Tags
widgets_values: white knit sweater, turtleneck, cleavage_cutout, boob window, pink plaid sweatpants, pink mouse slippers,

nodes.title: When to Rotate?
widgets_values:
Rotate when there are more than 1 characters, when all other options are exhausted. 
This occurs in complex poses, where the head isn't directly above the torso.

nodes.title: Megapizels can be ajusted
widgets_values:
If varying the see and re-rolling the Qwen Pose step does not improve the output, try adjusting the megapixels up or down a little. 

The default value is 1.5. The value should remain between 1.0 and 2.0.

nodes.title: Pose, Expression & Shot Tags
widgets_values: serene smile, heart gesture, heart hands, looking at viewer, smug, confident, :3, eyes half open, torso shot,

nodes.title: GGUF or AIO Qwen model?
widgets_values:
If you want to use the GGUF Qwen Image Edit 2511 models, which gives you choices in terms of size, but can't really do NSFW, you need to use the ORANGE set of models, which includes the GGUF Qwen model, the Qwen CLIP, the Qwen Vae, and the Qwen Lightning lora.

If you want to do NSFW stuff and your computer has the power, you can try loading the Qwen Rapid AIO (All-in-One) model, which includes the vae, CLIP and acceleration lora within.

Simply connect either the ORANGE or the GREEN models to the 3 small MAGENTA reroute nodes to the right. Easy Peasy!

nodes.title: Bookmarks (hotkey navigation)
widgets_values:
These are bookmarks linked to hotkeys. Press Number to automatically zoom to the area in question.

= - [CON] Bookmarks & Group Bypasser

1 - [CON] Prompt & Resolution

2 - [GEN] SDXL Pose

3 - [GEN] Qwen Pose

4 - [GEN] Qwen RePose

0 - [GEN] Info Sheet

nodes.title: Cache system
widgets_values:
The cache system essentially saves out an image into a cache folder each time you complete a group. Then, the subsequent group will fetch that cached image. The images are constantly being overwritten, so it won't implode your computer with intermediate images.

If you want to save out an intermediate (which, by the way, saves the workflow in the meta-data), each group has a preview image node, which you can click off the 'preview only' option.

Remember, you MUST update the full ComfuUI file path above for this to work. This is step #1!

nodes.title: Qwen Model Links
widgets_values:
## Model Links

**text_encoders**

- [qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/HunyuanVideo_1.5_repackaged/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors)

**loras**

- [Qwen-Image-Lightning-4steps-V2.0-bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/lightx2v/Qwen-Image-Lightning/blob/main/Qwen-Image-Lightning-4steps-V2.0-bf16.safetensors)
- [QIE_image2body_V2_dim4_1e-3-000078.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/tori29umai/QwenImageEdit2509_LoRA/blob/main/QIE_image2body_V2_dim4_1e-3-000078.safetensors)
- [QIE_charaBG_V8_dim4_1e-3-000048.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/tori29umai/QwenImageEdit2509_LoRA/blob/main/QIE_charaBG_V8_dim4_1e-3-000048.safetensors)
- Additional SDXL/Pony/Illustrious/Noob loras of your choice

**checkpoint**

- [WAI-illustrious-SDXL V16](https://civitai.com/models/827184/wai-illustrious-sdxl) (Any SDXL/Pony/Illustrious/Noob model will do. Depends on what you want and what loras you want to use)
- [v19/Qwen-Rapid-AIO-NSFW-v19.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Phr00t/Qwen-Image-Edit-Rapid-AIO/blob/main/v19/Qwen-Rapid-AIO-NSFW-v19.safetensors) (Any Qwen Rapid AIO NSFW model will do, I use v19. v23 is also recommended by the author.)


**diffusion_model**

- [qwen-image-edit-2511-Q8_0](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/Qwen-Image-Edit-2511-GGUF/blob/main/qwen-image-edit-2511-Q8_0.gguf) (Any Qwen Edit 2511 GGUF model will do, I use Q8_8. Q6_K is alright too.)

**vae**

- [qwen_image_vae.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/resolve/main/split_files/vae/qwen_image_vae.safetensors)
- [sdxl_vae.safetensors] (https://huggingface.co/stabilityai/sdxl-vae/blob/main/sdxl_vae.safetensors)


Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │      └── qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 loras/
│   │      |── Qwen-Image-Lightning-4steps-V2.0-bf16.safetensors
│   │      |── QIE_image2body_V2_dim4_1e-3-000078.safetensors
|   |      └── QIE_charaBG_V8_dim4_1e-3-000048.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 checkpoints/
│   │      |── Qwen-Rapid-AIO-NSFW-v19.safetensors
│   │      └── waiIllustriousSDXL_v160.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │      └── qwen-image-edit-2511-Q8_0.gguf
│   └── 📂 vae/
│          |── qwen_image_vae.safetensors
│          └── sdxl_vae.safetensors
```

nodes.title: Seed Control
widgets_values:
With seed fixed, keep tweaking the prompt till you arrive in the ballpark where you want in the SDXL Pose and Character.

Then, keep randomizing the seed till you get a result that you like, then fix the seed.

You push from the left of the workflow to the right. Bypass the groups that you are satisfied with the results. Rinse and repeat till you reach the end so you get the character posed in the way you want.

Many Groups will be able to zero-shot correct outputs. Playing seed gatcha is likely when the step is more complex.

I've found, gatcha is frequently needed for the SDXL Pose and Qwen RePose.

nodes.title: Workflow Usage
widgets_values:
Once the models and files are downloaded, the cache filepath are updated, and the prompts are ready, you're ready to run things.

IMPORTANT - TURN OFF groups you're not focused on! You should work on one group at a time!

Obviously, you can run the whole workflow or multiple groups, but there's a liklihood it will fetch the cache image from a previous run, so you need to run it again anyway.

This is the order you should push:
SDXL Pose
Qwen Pose
Qwen RePose
SDXL Refine

Each step has their quirks. If outputs are not zero shot, read the notes, there may be params to adjust.

nodes.title: Prompts
widgets_values:
This is for the prompts BUT ALSO loras for SDXL. Because some loras have trigger words and options, I decided to roll the SDXL loras in here.

Loras need to be in this file path format:
<lora:sdxl\slippy_magoo\Hot_Babe_Bazongas:1>

Otherwise, these loras are pretty self-explanatory, they are just split up.

Green prompts change the SDXL Character gen. They will only appear in SDXL refine step.

Blue prompts change the SDXL Pose gen.

Grey prompts change both.

If you don't want to change the character (consistent characters is the whole point of this workflow), then only alter the blue prompts.

The two deactivated prompts are for extra loras, one for character details, like clothing and accessories, and the other for the pose, which also includes expressions and hand gestures. SDXL Concept loras aplenty, go wild.

nodes.title: HOLD THE CHEESE!
widgets_values:
How do I get started?

Here's how!

Download all the models, put them in the right folders, hit ctrl+R to refresh, then load them into each model loader (magenta nodes).

The ONLY IMAGE, you need to load is the character sheet in the RED image load node over to the right.

You DO NEED to update the cache file path and maybe place the cache image files into the cache folder. These will be overwritten as you run the workflow.

Once you're done with that. Start putting in the SDXL prompts in the prompt group. Once your pose, character and mixed prompt tags are ready. You can start pushing through the workflow. Go read 'Workflow Usage' for specifics.
